prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='FT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds show high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.88) and high seasonality variance (0.77), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA dominates overall RMSE while FT_rf excels at seasonality, but the true series trend direction varies across folds (up-up-down). Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination with mixed weighting is optimal to leverage diverse model strengths.

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_FT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "FT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "ARIMA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "NaiveSeasonal", "late": "ARIMA"},
  "tier1_models": ["ARIMA", "THETA", "ETS", "CWT_svr", "DWT_svr", "FT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["rf", "NaiveSeasonal", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "catboost", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.8831,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds show high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.88) and high seasonality variance (0.77), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA dominates overall RMSE while FT_rf excels at seasonality, but the true series trend direction varies across folds (up-up-down). Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination with mixed weighting is optimal to leverage diverse model strengths."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds show high model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.88) and high seasonality variance (0.77), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA dominates overall RMSE while FT_rf excels at seasonality, but the true series trend direction varies across folds (up-up-down). Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination with mixed weighting is optimal to leverage diverse model strengths.

proposer.rationale: Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high_model_disagreement=true and high_seasonality_variance=0.77. n_unique_winners=8 indicates diverse per-horizon best models. ARIMA (RMSE=3715.46) dominates overall but trend varies across folds (up-up-down). Selection methods capture horizon-specific winners, weighted methods provide robustness to outliers with shrinkage=0.35, and DBA handles phase shifts. Score preset rmse_focus justified by RMSE spread=0.82 > 0.5 threshold.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No type has ≥4 candidates. All candidates are from valid_candidate_names and validated via anti-leakage expanding window (n_windows=3, rolling.mode=expanding). Universe leaderboard top candidates (ade_dynamic_error_beta0.8_trim0.6 score 0.737) are weaker than current set. No redundancy or leakage issues detected.
